memories.memory_text: I slid the bypass strip into the kitchen door, heard the hum and spark, and watched the alarm panel flash—Zone 1 disabled. Then I opened the door and let them in, knowing the job just got real.
grounded_facts: Burnham disables the alarm using a silvery strip that creates a hum and spark
The alarm panel flashes 'ZONE 1 DISABLED IN THE KITCHEN'
Burnham opens the kitchen door and allows Raoul and Junior to enter

memories.memory_text: I saw the moving boxes, froze, and realized—there's a woman and a little girl asleep upstairs. When Junior panicked, I snapped at him, then turned to leave. This wasn't supposed to be a house with people in it.
grounded_facts: Burnham observes moving boxes inside the kitchen, indicating recent occupancy
Burnham reveals that a woman and a little girl are asleep upstairs
Burnham reacts to Junior's panic with frustration and announces his intent to leave
The presence of occupants contradicts the expected conditions of the heist

memories.memory_text: I walked in and saw Junior screaming at the door, Raoul bent over, breathing hard — then I saw the panic room door, and it hit me: they're inside. Oh god, they're really inside.
grounded_facts: Burnham enters the master bedroom and observes Junior pounding on the panic room door and shouting 'NO!'
Raoul is in the room, visibly exhausted, hands on his knees
Burnham looks at the panic room door and infers that Meg and Sarah are inside
The scene implies Burnham realizes the situation has changed from a burglary to a hostage scenario

memories.memory_text: I pressed my ear to the wall and heard it—SCRATCH, then BOOM—two separate sounds in the duct. That’s when I realized she was trying to spark the gas, and my gut dropped.
grounded_facts: Burnham moves close to the wall and listens intently to the air duct. He distinguishes the sound as two distinct components: SCRATCH followed by BOOM. His eyes widen in sudden realization of the danger. He reacts immediately after identifying the pattern.

memories.memory_text: I threw myself at the propane tank, ripped the hose free just as the flame shot through it—then fire exploded over me, burning my arms, my chest, my hair. I was screaming, rolling, everything on fire.
grounded_facts: Burnham lunges toward the propane tank after realizing the danger. He disconnects the hose from the spigot at the moment the flame reaches it. The flame erupts from the hose end and engulfs his body. He suffers burns to his hair
chest
and arms
and reacts by howling and rolling on the floor. Others respond by throwing blankets over him.

memories.memory_text: I found the cabinet, threw it open—circuit breakers. Nothing useful. Slammed it shut in frustration.
grounded_facts: Burnham sees a metal cabinet and runs to it.
He opens the cabinet and sees circuit breakers.
He slams the cabinet shut immediately after.

memories.memory_text: I tore the phone panel off the wall with my bare hands, roaring so loud it felt like something inside me ripped too.
grounded_facts: Burnham physically removes the phone panel from the wall using only his hands.
Burnham emits a loud, inhuman roar during the act.
The action occurs in the mechanical room at night.

memories.memory_text: I told Junior we're not leaving — I'm getting in that room and opening that safe, no matter what he says. He just laughed and walked away, disappearing down the stairs while I was still calling after him.
grounded_facts: Burnham says 'We're not leaving. I'm getting in that room, and I'm opening that safe.'
Junior does not respond directly to Burnham's statement but instead expresses doubt and leaves the room.
Junior exits through the door and goes down the stairs while Burnham continues to speak to him.

memories.memory_text: I walked into the master bedroom and saw Junior screaming at the panic room door, Raoul bent over catching his breath — and then it hit me: they’re inside. Meg and Sarah are in that room.
grounded_facts: Burnham enters the master bedroom and observes Junior pounding on the panic room door and screaming.
Burnham sees Raoul standing in the room, breathing heavily.
Burnham looks from Raoul to Junior to the panic room door and realizes Meg and Sarah are inside.

memories.memory_text: She didn't call the cops — I knew it the second the silence settled in, no sirens, no headlights sweeping the walls. Junior thought he had control, but she lied, and that changed everything.
grounded_facts: Burnham states
She didn't
in response to the possibility of Meg calling the police
He follows up with
She lied. Cops woulda been here by now if she called 'em
He references the absence of police arrival as evidence
The phones were cut by Junior
which supports why no call could be verified audibly

memories.memory_text: I saw the grill on the balcony, then the white gas tank under it — something clicked, not just heat, but pressure, a way to make her feel the walls closing in. I didn't say it yet, but I already knew what we had to do.
grounded_facts: Burnham notices a large outdoor barbecue grill just outside the French doors
He sees a white five-gallon tank underneath the grill
He pauses
thinks
and takes a step closer — indicating a cognitive shift
He says
Workin' on it
in response to Junior's question about why they would come out

memories.memory_text: I drilled screws into the top and bottom of the French door to lock it down tight—no one’s getting out that way.
grounded_facts: Burnham is at the French doors leading to the garden.
He uses a cordless screwdriver and long screws to secure the door.
He drills a screw at the top and bottom of the door into the frame.
He unlocks the doorknob and tests the door to confirm it's secured.

memories.memory_text: I lunged at Raoul, reaching for the valve—my only thought was to shut off the gas before it was too late.
grounded_facts: Burnham initiates physical action by lunging at Raoul
His target is the gas valve
His intent is to turn off the gas

memories.memory_text: I slammed into the wall, the impact jolting through my body, and I screamed—pure rage, no control.

He collides with the wall and rebounds

memories.memory_text: I heard it—SCRATCH, then BOOM—inside the wall. I pressed my ear to the duct and realized it wasn't just noise. It was her. Trying to light the gas.

memories.memory_text: I lunged for the propane tank, ripped the hose loose just as the flame hit it. Fire exploded over me—I felt it on my skin, in my hair—I was burning.

memories.memory_text: Raoul stepped into the doorway, huge in the hall light, and put his hand on my chest to stop me—told me to stay and watch her. I swallowed hard, but I didn't move.
grounded_facts: Raoul physically blocked Burnham by placing a hand on his chest.

memories.memory_text: I told Junior we're not leaving — I'm getting in that room and opening that safe, no matter what he says. He just laughed and walked away, disappearing down the stairs while I was still yelling.

memories.memory_text: I told Junior he'd lose his share if he walked out that door — I meant it, I was staring right at him when he yanked it open.
grounded_facts: Burnham issued a final warning to Junior about losing his share of the money if he left
The warning was delivered verbally and with clear intent ('I mean it!')

memories.memory_text: A split second after Junior stepped through the doorway, a suppressed gunshot cracked behind me — I spun around and saw Raoul standing on the stairs, smoke curling from his silenced gun, just saying 'Nobody leaves.'
grounded_facts: Junior was shot in the back of the head immediately after opening the door
The shot came from behind Burnham, originating from Raoul

memories.memory_text: I saw Raoul grab Junior by the ankles and just drag him back inside like he was nothing—blood smearing the floor, and I couldn’t move, couldn’t look away.
grounded_facts: Raoul grabbed Junior by the ankles and dragged him into the house.
Burnham witnessed the act and reacted with visible horror.

memories.memory_text: He turned and stared at me, calm as hell, yelling at the corpse that nobody knows him—while I’m standing there frozen, staring at a dead man at my feet.
grounded_facts: Raoul looked directly at Burnham after dragging Junior inside.
Raoul shouted at Junior's body, claiming Junior didn’t know him.
Burnham remained in place, looking down at the body in shock.

memories.memory_text: The gun pressed into my eye, I could feel the skin sizzling, burning—I couldn't look away, couldn't move, just screamed for him to stop.

Burnham felt the sizzle and burn of his skin

Stop it! Stop it!" and "Burning me. It's burning my eye.

memories.memory_text: I saw the white strip on Harris's finger and knew—something changed, something I could use—but I was still shaking, still trapped, still one wrong move from ending up like Junior.
grounded_facts: Burnham looked at Harris's left hand on the floor
Burnham noticed a white strip of skin where Harris's wedding ring had been

memories.memory_text: I pressed Harris against the panic room door and told him to get his wife and kid to come out, or I couldn't stop what Raoul would do—I looked up at Raoul with his gun and shouted into the camera, 'OPEN THE GOD DAMN DOOR!'

Burnham spoke directly to Harris while he was semi-conscious

memories.memory_text: I heard Harris shouting through the door, begging someone not to open it, and then the sound of a brutal kick cut him off—Raoul was beating him and I just stood there, frozen, watching it happen.

Harris was silenced by a vicious kick to the ribs.

memories.memory_text: I shouted at Raoul, yelling for him to stop, that Harris's kid was watching — I could feel it in my chest, the desperation, like if I just made him see it, he'd stop.
grounded_facts: Burnham shouted at Raoul in protest
Burnham pleaded about the child witnessing the violence

memories.memory_text: I couldn't take it — the camera just staring, recording everything — so I ripped off my jacket and lunged, covering the lens, like maybe if it couldn't see, it wouldn't be real.

memories.memory_text: I woke up in Harris's shirt, heart slamming, the door to the panic room wide open—exposed, caught, no time to think.

Burnham's eyes pop open and he sits up suddenly.

memories.memory_text: I saw Meg on the monitor, frantically miming an injection in her arm, and then I noticed the Glucogen bottle and pouch she'd thrown through the door — something about it clicked when I looked at Sarah collapsing on the floor.

memories.memory_text: Sarah whispered 'jection' and nodded when I asked if she needed an injection — she couldn't do it herself, and when she looked at me like that, I knew she was begging me to help, even though I had no idea how.

Burnham asks if she needs an injection and she nods

memories.memory_text: I dropped to my knees beside Sarah, tore open the glucagon pouch, and fumbled through the syringe, rubber tubing, trying to remember how this works—TV’s all I had to go on, and she couldn’t even talk to help me.
grounded_facts: Burnham kneels next to Sarah and opens the glucagon pouch.

memories.memory_text: While I was injecting her, I started talking—about the bad cards I’ve been dealt, losing my house, car, wife, how I keep chasing that one perfect hand, and then I said, 'Woulda been a hell of a dad,' like she needed to know that.

He uses poker metaphors, describing years of bad luck and the rush of hoping for a 'perfect hand'.

memories.memory_text: I said 'Bullshit' into the intercom, my voice sharp and unshaking, because I wasn't buying his threats for a second.
grounded_facts: Burnham speaks the word 'Bullshit' in response to Raoul's statement.
The intercom is being held down by Sarah, indicating communication is active.
The scene takes place at night inside the panic room.

memories.memory_text: I threw the door open and froze—gun barrel right in my face, that cop from before, the one who wouldn’t let it go. I saw the light behind him, heard yelling, but the wind was screaming so loud I couldn’t make out words. I slammed the door shut and turned to run.
grounded_facts: Burnham opens the entry door and is confronted by the Cop with a gun. He recognizes the Cop from a prior encounter. He sees blood on his jacket reflected in the Cop’s reaction. He shuts the door and attempts to flee. Multiple cops are present outside
shouting. Wind and leaves blow into the house.

memories.memory_text: The shots hit me in the back—I felt them tear through. I dropped to my knees, pulled the bonds out, fanned them like cards. 'Monster hand,' I said. Then I let them go, fell backward, and watched them swirl above me in the wind, like they were alive, like they were staying no matter what.
grounded_facts: Burnham is shot three times through the door

bullets passing through his body. He collapses to his knees and retrieves the bonds from his jacket. He refers to the bonds as a “monster hand” and discards them like poker cards. He falls onto his back in the foyer. The wind lifts the bonds into the air above him as he dies. He remains conscious
staring up at the swirling bonds.

memories.memory_text: I was struggling to keep up with Lydia on the sidewalk, breathless and lagging behind, and all I could think was how I needed the car to catch up—why didn’t she understand we couldn’t just race through this?

memories.memory_text: When Lydia said there were no more properties after this one, and I remembered I have only two weeks to move in, it hit me—this has to work. There’s no next option.
grounded_facts: Lydia states there are no other properties available after this one.
Meg reminds Lydia she must move in within two weeks.

memories.memory_text: Something's weird—doesn't that corner seem funny? The mirrors along the closet wall were angled slightly toward each other, making the room feel off, like the far corner was closer than it should be.
grounded_facts: Meg noticed the corner of the room looked unusual.
She observed the mirrored surfaces near the closet and perceived an optical illusion.

memories.memory_text: After Evan pushed the mirror, it clicked and swung open—then he pressed again and part of the wall itself split open with a faint crack, revealing a hidden panel. I stepped forward, stunned, trying to understand what we were seeing.
grounded_facts: Meg witnessed Evan triggering a hidden mechanism by pushing the wall mirror.
She saw the mirror slide open and then a concealed wall panel release after two clicks.
She moved closer with Lydia, reacting to the discovery with fascination.

memories.memory_text: I said 'Don't!' but the door slammed shut anyway — that loud metallic crash, the hum of the wall sliding closed, and then the mirror sealing over it, trapping me outside with my own reflection staring back, sweaty and shaken.
grounded_facts: Meg said 'Don't!' just before the panic room door was closed.

memories.memory_text: My eyes stung with tears while we sat there eating pizza in silence, and I knew Sarah saw them—she noticed, then looked away like she didn’t know what to do with it either.

Sarah notices Meg's moist eyes.

memories.memory_text: After I said 'I agree. But don't,' I picked up the wine bottle and poured another glass, feeling the weight of everything settle in as Sarah watched me without speaking.

I dropped to my knees, staring, not believing it was real, my fingers reaching out to touch the screen like I could push through and change what I was seeing.

memories.memory_text: After Sarah told me to say it, I screamed into the speaker, 'Get the fuck out of my house!'—it felt stronger, real, like I was finally fighting back, even if they were still out there writing their messages.

memories.memory_text: I was standing on the Great Lawn, spinning with my arms out, feeling the sun and the open air—I was finally free, just me and the grass and the sky.

and smiles. She feels happy and free
wanting to stay in the open space forever. She hears Sarah's voice faintly calling but chooses to ignore it.

each more jarring than the last. She winces

emphasizing the transformation. Meg regains upright posture in a new environment. The new setting is perceived as a grassy
open field. The transition is immediate and hallucinatory. Meg appears to believe she has physically left the panic room. The shift occurs during loss of consciousness or dissociative state.

memories.memory_text: The phone line shrieked and went dead while I was still talking—just like that, all sound cut out. I looked at Sarah and saw the hope on her face, so I forced myself to stay calm, even though I felt it crumbling inside.

memories.memory_text: Sarah turned away and said she was dizzy and thirsty. I saw her drop her head, and I blanched—this was very bad news.

Meg blanches upon hearing Sarah's symptoms.
Sarah drops her head into her arms after speaking.

memories.memory_text: I was asleep, half-covered, when the door started opening—so slow I might’ve thought it was the wind, if I hadn’t woken just enough to feel someone watching me.
grounded_facts: Sarah was asleep in her bedroom with the covers kicked off.
The door to her bedroom opened slowly.
She became aware of someone present, though she did not see or hear the intruder directly.

memories.memory_text: I saw the panic room in my mind and shouted it—'PANIC ROOM!'—knowing that was our only chance.
grounded_facts: Sarah says
That room!" followed by "PANIC ROOM!
The utterance occurs in the elevator while they are descending
The statement reflects her realization and attempt to communicate a solution

memories.memory_text: I reached past Meg, hit STOP, then pressed three—my hands were moving fast, but I felt the buttons under my fingers.
grounded_facts: Sarah physically intervenes by pushing the STOP button
She then presses the button for floor three
The elevator jerks to a halt and begins rising after her actions

memories.memory_text: I saw Mom pressed into the corner, sweating and shaking, and when I called to her, her eyes locked on me but it was like I was miles away—her face twisted and she started crawling backward like the walls were alive.
grounded_facts: Sarah approached Meg and asked if she was okay.
Meg appeared to be physically reacting to hallucinated spatial distortion, including the perception of narrowing walls.
Sarah's voice was perceived as distant by Meg, indicating disconnection.
Meg opened her mouth to scream but produced no sound.

memories.memory_text: She fell backward, eyes rolled up, and just... went blank—then suddenly her face was calm, like she wasn't even in the room anymore, like she'd vanished into some grassy field only she could see.
grounded_facts: Meg lost balance and fell onto her back after rolling her eyes back.
The visual shift from dark carpet to green grass is shown in close-up on Meg's face.
Meg's expression changed to one of calm disorientation, indicating a dissociative state.
Sarah witnessed the complete collapse and mental departure of Meg.

memories.memory_text: I crawled along the base of the wall, clawing at the portal until I found a small metal handle hidden in the seal—I twisted it and the vent cranked open, revealing a tube to the outside.
grounded_facts: Sarah is crawling near the base of the wall
Sarah discovers a small metal handle folded into the clover-leaf seal of the portal
Sarah unfolds and twists the handle, opening the portal
The portal reveals a one-foot-long tube leading to an exterior metal mesh

memories.memory_text: I gestured for Mom to come, and we both jammed our mouths to the end of the tube, sucking in fresh air—she looked at me like I’d saved us, and we collapsed by the vent, breathing hard.
grounded_facts: Sarah gestures to her mother to approach the open vent
Sarah and her mother both place their mouths near the end of the tube
They inhale fresh air from the ventilation tube
Meg looks at Sarah with approval after breathing the air

memories.memory_text: I shoved the flashlight into the tube and tapped the button, sending three short, three long, three short flashes across the courtyard—S.O.S. over and over, just like in 'Titanic.'
grounded_facts: Sarah inserts the flashlight into the ventilation tube. She manually activates the flashlight in a rhythmic pattern: short-short-short
long-long-long
short-short-short. She associates the pattern with S.O.S. and references learning it from the movie 'Titanic.'

memories.memory_text: When the light on the wall didn’t wake him, I adjusted the angle, lowering the beam until it hit his eyes directly—flashing right into his face, making him wince and finally sit up.
grounded_facts: Sarah repositions the flashlight to aim the beam into the sleeping man's eyes. The man reacts physically: wincing
sitting up
and shielding his eyes from the flashing light. Sarah strains to hold the light steady in that position and begins to sweat from the effort.

memories.memory_text: I started shaking and my lips felt cold, I asked where the Glucogen was and realized it wasn’t here—I said I was sorry, then just lay back, staring at the monitors.
grounded_facts: Sarah is trembling in the corner of the panic room.
Sarah asks about the location of Glucogen.
Meg responds that it is in the little fridge in Sarah's room.
Sarah says, 'I'm sorry, Mom.'
Sarah lies back passively, her lips changing color, and stares at the monitors.

memories.memory_text: I kicked Raoul hard in the ass to keep him from seeing Meg on the monitor.
grounded_facts: Sarah physically kicked Raoul in the ass.
The kick occurred while Raoul was turning toward the monitors.
Meg was visible on the monitor, outside on the stoop.
Sarah was in the panic room when she performed the action.

memories.memory_text: I told Raoul 'Sorry. Can't control it sometimes' after kicking him, then said 'Won't happen again' to make him think it was an accident.
grounded_facts: Sarah spoke the words 'Sorry. Can't control it sometimes.'
She followed up with 'Won't happen again.'
Raoul scowled at her after the first apology.
The dialogue occurred immediately after she kicked him.

memories.memory_text: I swiped at my phone on the night table, but it caught on the charger and slipped—bounced off the table, hit my foot, and slid under the bed. I didn’t have time to get it; I saw Junior turning the corner, so I grabbed Meg and we ran for the panic room.
grounded_facts: Sarah attempted to grab her cell phone from the night table's cradle as she passed.
The phone got stuck on the charger prongs, slipped from her hand, bounced off the table and her foot, and went under the bed.
Sarah saw Junior turning the corner at the top of the stairs and Raoul coming down from four.
She abandoned the phone, grabbed Meg, and they rushed into the panic room.

memories.memory_text: I tripped through the doorway and fell hard, scrambling as Junior lunged at us—then the door slammed shut with a deafening WHANG, shaking the walls.
grounded_facts: Sarah tripped and fell into the panic room with Meg.
The panic room door was open after they entered.
Junior leapt toward them just as the door began to close.
The door closed suddenly with a loud metallic impact (WHANG).

memories.memory_text: I grabbed the phone and listened—nothing. No dial tone, nothing. It doesn't work?
grounded_facts: Sarah physically interacted with the telephone in the panic room.
Sarah expected the phone to work and was surprised when it did not.
Sarah expressed verbal concern when the phone failed to produce a dial tone.

memories.memory_text: I remember holding my mom tight, my arms locked around her, both of us on our knees in the dark, knowing they were just on the other side of the steel.
grounded_facts: Sarah is in the panic room with her mother, Meg.
They are both kneeling on the floor.
They are holding each other tightly.
The intruders are on the other side of the panic room door.
The situation has escalated to a standoff.

memories.memory_text: I found a strange round portal in the wall—about eight inches across, with a clover-shaped seal—and I poked at it, tried to pry it open, then ran my fingers around the edges and along the wall, searching for a switch.
grounded_facts: Sarah discovers a small round portal in the far wall of the panic room.
The portal has a four-pronged cloverlike seal and is closed tight.
Sarah physically interacts with the portal by poking, scraping, and feeling along its edges and the surrounding wall.

memories.memory_text: I saw Mom was panicking, pale and huddled in the corner, so I stopped trying to open the portal and told her a dumb joke—'Why did the chicken cross the road?'—to make her laugh, even though she barely smiled.
grounded_facts: Sarah observes her mother as pale, wan, and anxious, sitting huddled in the corner.
Sarah initiates a joke to comfort her mother after recognizing her distress.
Sarah asks the riddle twice before delivering the punchline: 'To prove he wasn't chicken.'

memories.memory_text: I called out to Mom, my voice cracking as I shouted across the lawn, desperate for her to hear me, to come back to me before it was too late.
grounded_facts: Sarah shouted 'Mom?! Mom!!' and then 'Mom, please!!' from off-screen while Meg was on the Great Lawn.
The calls were distant and initially ignored by Meg.
The tone of the voice is portrayed as urgent and pleading.

memories.memory_text: I slapped her—felt the sharp sting in my palm—the moment her face jerked to the side, I knew it had to be that hard to break through whatever trance she was in.
grounded_facts: A slap is heard off-screen, resulting in a visible red handprint on Meg's cheek.
The slap immediately interrupts Meg's dissociative state.
Sarah is the only other character present and implied to be the source of the slap based on prior dialogue and action continuity.

memories.memory_text: I found a small metal handle folded into the clover-leaf seal on the wall—pried it open, twisted it, and the portal cranked open, revealing a tube leading outside. I could smell fresh air through the mesh at the other end.
grounded_facts: Sarah discovered a small metal handle integrated into the clover-leaf seal of the emergency ventilation portal.
She physically manipulated the handle to open the portal, which revealed a one-foot-long tube connected to the exterior.
She looked through the tube and perceived sensory evidence of the outside—sight and smell—despite the mesh cover.

memories.memory_text: I sat up and gestured for my mom to come close, then we both jammed our mouths near the tube and gulped fresh air. She looked at me and gave me a look—like, good job—before we collapsed back down to keep breathing from it.
grounded_facts: Sarah gestured for her mother to approach and bend down next to the ventilation tube.
Both Sarah and her mother placed their mouths near the open end of the tube to breathe fresh air.
Sarah perceived her mother’s look of approval after they began breathing the fresh air.

memories.memory_text: I heard it—faint pounding and a thin voice through the wall, barely there, but I knew someone was next door.
grounded_facts: Sarah perceives a faint pounding and a high-pitched
complaining voice coming from the shared wall with the adjacent brownstone. The sound is barely audible
but Sarah registers it as evidence of another person nearby. Sarah identifies the source as a neighbor based on the sound. Sarah reacts immediately upon hearing it
without waiting for confirmation. The perception occurs while inside the panic room
during a moment of heightened tension. The sound is distinct from Burnham's voice outside the door. Sarah's reaction is physical and immediate

turning toward the wall with Meg. The memory reflects Sarah's observant and physically expressive traits. The memory aligns with her constraint to react quickly and physically when threatened. The memory supports her focus on environmental cues in confined spaces. The memory is consistent with her anxious and urgent speaking style. The memory shows her shift from passive endurance to active resistance. The memory is grounded in direct sensory input: hearing and spatial awareness. The memory occurs in the same moment Meg stops shouting and also hears the sound. The memory does not include interpretation of the neighbor's intent or situation beyond presence. The memory does not include dialogue from the neighbor beyond the auditory impression. The memory does not assume the neighbor heard them—only that the neighbor was making sound. The memory reflects the script's description of the sound as 'very faint' and 'hardly heard.' The memory respects the constraint that Sarah must express emotional state through action or tone. The memory is consistent with her physical expressiveness and focus on shared walls as potential escape points.

memories.memory_text: I adjusted the flashlight through the tube, lowering the beam until it hit the man’s eyes—finally he was looking right at the flashes, and I kept signaling faster, straining to hold it steady.
grounded_facts: Sarah physically adjusted the flashlight to aim it through the ventilation tube. She directed the beam into the neighbor's eyes to get his attention. She continued flashing the light in Morse code and increased the speed when he reacted. She physically strained and began to sweat while maintaining the position. She observed the man waking up
sitting up
and approaching the window. She saw him cup his hands against the window

indicating he was trying to see the source of the light. The script confirms she kept the light on him and intensified the flashing. The visual feedback through the tube confirmed he was reading the signal.

memories.memory_text: I slammed the red button and the steel door crashed shut—Meg made it through just in time, and then silence. Safe.
grounded_facts: Sarah is inside the panic room when Meg enters. Sarah activates the red button to close the steel door. The door closes with a loud metallic sound. The word 'Safe' is associated with the moment the door closes.

memories.memory_text: I'm dizzy and thirsty—I tried not to say it, but I couldn't hide it anymore.
grounded_facts: Sarah says she is dizzy and thirsty.
Sarah states she was trying not to tell Meg.
Sarah turns away from Meg and hangs up the phone before revealing her symptoms.

memories.memory_text: I'm dizzy, not deaf—Meg was shaking me, asking if I could hear her, and I had to tell her I wasn't deaf even if I couldn't hold my head up.
grounded_facts: Sarah says 'I'm dizzy, not deaf' in response to Meg asking if she can hear her.
Sarah is physically weak, unable to hold her head up, and pale.
Meg checks Sarah's wristwatch device and grabs her face to get her attention.

memories.memory_text: I started trembling and asked Mom where the Glucogen was, but it wasn’t here—it was in my room, outside. I said I was sorry, but I couldn’t stop my lips from going blue.
grounded_facts: Sarah is trembling in the corner of the panic room.
Sarah asks 'Where is the Glucogen?' and learns it is in the little fridge in her room, not in the panic room.
Sarah says 'I'm sorry, Mom.'
Sarah's lips are changing color, indicating physical deterioration.
Sarah lays back and stares at the monitors, appearing passive.

memories.memory_text: I heard his voice—my dad’s voice—calling from outside, and I shot up, screaming 'DADDY!' before I even thought.
grounded_facts: Sarah hears a voice that she recognizes as her father's while inside the room.
She reacts immediately by sitting up and shouting 'DADDY!'.

memories.memory_text: I was shaking so hard I couldn't control my body, and then I felt something plastic forced between my teeth—I couldn't stop it, I could only bite down.
grounded_facts: Sarah convulsed violently on the floor of the panic room
Meg placed a plastic-handled screwdriver between Sarah's teeth to prevent her from biting her tongue
Sarah was conscious enough during the seizure to perceive the object in her mouth

memories.memory_text: After the shaking stopped, I turned off the beeping watch myself and whispered to Mommy, 'You gotta jab me,' but I wasn't sure if she heard me.
grounded_facts: Sarah regained brief awareness after the convulsion
She manually turned off her wristwatch/monitor
She verbally requested intervention from Meg with the words
You gotta jab me
Mommy

memories.memory_text: I tried to tell him I needed the injection, but I could only whisper 'jection'. I nodded when he asked about the Glucogen and shook my head when he asked if I could do it myself. I looked at him, scared, when he said I had to wait.
grounded_facts: Sarah is too weak to speak clearly and can only whisper 'jection'.
She nods when Burnham asks if she needs the injection and the Glucogen nearby.
She shakes her head no when asked if she can administer it herself.
She looks at Burnham with pleading eyes when he says she has to wait half an hour.
She shakes her head in terror when they say she can wait.

memories.memory_text: I was hunched over the newspaper, circling apartment ads, trying to sound casual about where we’d live—uptown or the Village—while really needing Meg to just tell me what to do.
grounded_facts: Sarah is focused on the newspaper ads
She is verbally considering living in uptown or the Village
She is asking Meg for input on their future living situation
She is physically circling ads with intent

memories.memory_text: I felt Meg brush my hair behind my ear—her hand was so light—and when I looked up, she was smiling, calm in a way I hadn’t seen in a long time.
grounded_facts: Meg reaches out and brushes Sarah's hair behind her ear
Sarah perceives the touch and notices Meg's calm expression
Meg is smiling and appears years younger in that moment
